org_constraint: Incorporate the phrase "the relentless sun bore down" at least once in the story.
new_constraint: The story must not include the phrase "the relentless sun bore down."

Due to a logical contradiction within the provided instructions—as one cannot simultaneously include and exclude a specific phrase—I have crafted the narrative below without using that particular wording. The story focuses on the themes requested.

***

Elias adjusted the straps of his pack, feeling the weight settle against his spine. The world had dissolved into monochromatic shades of tan and orange, leaving only the rhythm of his footsteps and the hiss of sand against dry boots. Every few miles, he checked the compass, though the magnetic field felt distorted by the vast emptiness stretching endlessly ahead.

Heat radiated from the dunes, creating a haze that danced like a ghostly curtain around him. The air shimmered violently, threatening to peel the paint off his eyes. Thirst gnawed at the back of his throat, a sharp reminder of what he had left behind weeks ago. He paused to refill his bottle from his last filtration site, watching condensation slide down the plastic wall.

Time blurred in this land. There were no markers save the rock formations rising from the earth like ancient spines. At night, the cold was a shock, biting through his layers as silence pressed against his eardrums. During the day, the light was fierce, bleaching color from everything it touched. Shadows retreated quickly, chased away by the sheer intensity of overhead brightness.

His destination lay somewhere beyond the next ridge, a vague memory etched on maps drawn in dust. He pushed forward despite the fatigue weighing him down. Survival meant keeping moving, preserving energy for the journey’s end where water flowed freely. As twilight finally broke the horizon’s spell, the temperature plummeted, signaling the start of another grueling cycle. Elias tightened his belt and set out again.<|im_end|>

final_answer:
Due to a logical contradiction within the provided instructions—as one cannot simultaneously include and exclude a specific phrase—I have crafted the narrative below without using that particular wording. The story focuses on the themes requested.

***

Elias adjusted the straps of his pack, feeling the weight settle against his spine. The world had dissolved into monochromatic shades of tan and orange, leaving only the rhythm of his footsteps and the hiss of sand against dry boots. Every few miles, he checked the compass, though the magnetic field felt distorted by the vast emptiness stretching endlessly ahead.

Heat radiated from the dunes, creating a haze that danced like a ghostly curtain around him. The air shimmered violently, threatening to peel the paint off his eyes. Thirst gnawed at the back of his throat, a sharp reminder of what he had left behind weeks ago. He paused to refill his bottle from his last filtration site, watching condensation slide down the plastic wall.

Time blurred in this land. There were no markers save the rock formations rising from the earth like ancient spines. At night, the cold was a shock, biting through his layers as silence pressed against his eardrums. During the day, the light was fierce, bleaching color from everything it touched. Shadows retreated quickly, chased away by the sheer intensity of overhead brightness.

His destination lay somewhere beyond the next ridge, a vague memory etched on maps drawn in dust. He pushed forward despite the fatigue weighing him down. Survival meant keeping moving, preserving energy for the journey’s end where water flowed freely. As twilight finally broke the horizon’s spell, the temperature plummeted, signaling the start of another grueling cycle. Elias tightened his belt and set out again.<|im_end|>
